content.full_text:
DAVID BAUER BUSINESS SERVICES
1121 L Street, Suite 1000
Sacramento, CA 95814
Tel (916) 447-4489 · Fax (916) 441-6639

May 4, 1990

Kathryn Donovan
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J St., 8th Floor
Sacramento CA 95814

Dear Ms Donovan:

Mr. Wayne Emberrv recommended that I request a written opinion
from the commission on how to handle the following situation.

California State Senator John Doolittle is currently running for
U. S. Congress. The John T. Doolittle for Congress committee has
scheduled a fundraising event for later this month. It is
intended that all permissible (noncorporate) contributions
resulting from the event will be accepted by the federal committee
and deposited into its bank account.

It is likely that the event will prompt contributions to the
senator's state committee from organizations which are prohibited
from donating to federal committees. To avoid an in-kind
contribution situation resulting from the federal committee's
paying all of the costs of the event, we felt that it would be
appropriate for the state committee to pay a portion of the bills
based on its share of the gross proceeds.

I called Mr. Emberrv to explain the situation and, after
consulting with others in his section, he suggested that the state
committee should pay one half of the costs associated with the
event. We expect the bulk of the proceeds to be deposited into
the federal committee, as the invitations specifically request
that contributions be made to the John T. Doolittle for Congress
committee. It is possible, in fact, that the state committee's
proceeds will be less than 50% of the costs, although the total
proceeds will certainly exceed the costs. That disproportionate
allocation of the costs presents a problem in that the federal
committee could then be receiving an in-kind contribution from the
state committee. By itself, that is not a problem. However, the
inkind contribution will have been financed with contributions
which are impermissible for federal committees--the very reason
they would go to the state committee in the first place.

As I mentioned earlier, the event is scheduled for later this
month. If an opinion could be expedited in any way, I would
appreciate it. Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

David Bauer, Treasurer
Friends of John Doolittle Candidate Committee, Id. #810028

[The image appears to be a very faded or low-contrast document with text that is largely illegible. Most of the content cannot be read clearly enough to transcribe accurately. The document appears to be a multi-paragraph text, possibly typed or printed, but the specific words and content are not sufficiently visible to provide a reliable transcription while following the requirement to transcribe exactly what is seen.]

California State Senator John Doolittle is currently running for U.S. Congress. The John T. Doolittle for Congress committee has scheduled a fundraising event for later this month. It is intended that all permissible (noncorporate) contributions resulting from the event will be accepted by the federal committee and deposited into its bank account. It is likely that the event will prompt contributions to the senator's state committee from organizations which are prohibited from donating to federal committees. To avoid an in-kind contribution situation resulting from the federal committee's paying all of the costs of the event, we felt that it would be appropriate for the state committee to pay a portion of the bills based on its share of the gross proceeds. I called Mr. Emberry to explain the situation and, after consulting with others in his section, he suggested that the state committee should pay one half of the costs associated with the event. We expect the bulk of the proceeds to be deposited into the federal committee, as the invitations specifically request that contributions be made to the John T. Doolittle for Congress committee. It is possible, in fact, that the state committee's proceeds will be less than 50% of the costs, although the total proceeds will certainly exceed the costs. That disproportionate allocation of the costs presents a problem in that the federal committee could then be receiving an in-kind contribution from the state committee. By itself, that is not a problem. However, the inkind contribution will have been financed with contributions which are impermissible for federal committees—the very reason they would go to the state committee in the first place. As I mentioned earlier, the event is scheduled for later this month. If an opinion could be expedited in any way, I would appreciate it. Thank you for your assistance. Sincerely, David Bauer, Treasurer Friends of John Doolittle Candidate Committee, Id. #810028 It seems that the image contains mostly white text on a very light background, making it extremely faint and difficult to read clearly. If you have another scan or higher quality image, please provide it for better results.
